title: luzula multiflora subsp. calabra
intro: Luzula multiflora subsp. calabra, also known as Calabrian woodrush, is a plant species within the sedge family that is native to the Calabrian Peninsula in southern Italy. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which provide its medicinal properties. Calabrian woodrush has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. It can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, or salves, allowing individuals to harness its benefits for a range of health concerns.
